---
title: "00 · Kiselev — Algebra, Part II"
subtitle: "Irrational and complex numbers, higher-degree equations, exponentials and logarithms, sequences and progressions, combinatorics and the binomial theorem, limits — the classical Russian second course."
---

## What this tier is for

This is the **Kiselev Algebra, Part II** track — the natural
continuation of Part I ([`ka1`](../tier-kiselev-1-algebra-1/index.qmd)).
Part II is where school algebra opens up: numbers escape the rationals,
sequences extend to infinity, and the apparatus of limits is laid down
just deeply enough to read calculus.

By the end of Part II you can:

- Reason about **irrational** and **complex** numbers as legitimate
  inhabitants of the number system, not exotic visitors.
- Solve and analyse **higher-degree equations** and small systems of
  them.
- Work with **inequalities of higher degree** and absolute values.
- Manipulate **exponentials and logarithms** as inverses, including
  change of base and the asymptotic behaviour.
- Sum **arithmetic and geometric progressions** — finite and infinite
  — and recognise convergence.
- Count with **permutations and combinations** and expand with the
  **binomial theorem**.
- Read the early language of **limits** and **continuity** without
  needing the full calculus.

## Source

Russian original (public domain on archive.org):
[Алгебра. Часть вторая, А. П. Киселёв](https://archive.org/details/kiselyov-algebra-part-2-2005).
The PDF stays external; the pointers in each lesson cite the relevant
Russian sections.

## Lessons

The full Algebra II track, in book order:

1. [**01 · Complex Numbers (Ch 1)**](01-complex-numbers/index.qmd) —
   $i^2 = -1$, arithmetic on $a + bi$, modulus, conjugate.
2. [**02 · Higher-Degree Equations (Ch 2)**](02-higher-degree-equations/index.qmd) —
   rational-root theorem, Cardano, biquadratic, Fundamental Theorem
   of Algebra.
3. [**03 · Higher-Degree Inequalities (Ch 3)**](03-higher-degree-inequalities/index.qmd) —
   sign-charts, rational inequalities, AM-GM.
4. [**04 · Exponentials and Logarithms (Ch 4)**](04-exponentials-logarithms/index.qmd) —
   $a^x$, $\log_a x$, three log laws, change of base.
5. [**05 · Sequences and Progressions (Ch 5)**](05-sequences-progressions/index.qmd) —
   AP, GP, infinite GP convergence; means and AM-GM-HM.
6. [**06 · Combinatorics and the Binomial Theorem (Ch 6)**](06-combinatorics-binomial/index.qmd) —
   permutations, combinations, Pascal's triangle, $(a + b)^n$.
7. [**07 · Limits and Continued Fractions (Ch 7)**](07-limits/index.qmd) —
   sequence limits, the squeeze theorem, $e$ as a limit,
   continued fractions.

**Track complete.** This closes the four-tier Kiselev geometry +
algebra track.

## Sister synthesis tiers

The synthesis lessons covering the same ground sit in
[Tier 3 Algebra II / Geometry / Trig](../tier-3-algebra2-geometry-trig/index.qmd)
(quadratic formula, sequences and series, logarithms, combinatorics,
binomial theorem) and the more advanced
[Tier 6 Undergrad Advanced](../tier-6-undergrad-advanced/index.qmd)
(complex numbers and complex functions). The CBSE Class 11 track
([Class 11](../tier-cbse-11-class-11/index.qmd)) covers the same
material under board-exam discipline.
